name: Hindi
register: Reverent contemporary Hindi in the tradition of the Bible Society of India पवित्र बाइबिल (the beloved Hindi O.V. and its revision), which most Hindi-speaking congregations pray and read from. Vocabulary is the familiar church register — dignified but warm, accessible Devanagari, not high-Sanskritized literary Hindi.
quote_convention: Modern Hindi print convention: double quotation marks “…” for direct speech, single ‘…’ for a quote inside a quote. Devanagari carries no case, so reverence is marked by word choice and the honorific -जी / हे-vocative, not capitalization.
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Proverbs, prophetic oracles) is set as line-broken verse with the second line of each parallel couplet indented, matching the stanza layout of the Hindi Bible; prose paragraphs run full-measure.

glossary.note: The exact vocative of the Hindi Lord's Prayer (हे हमारे स्वर्गीय पिता) — instantly recognized as a child's address, never a title for deity.

Hindi reviewer feedback 2026-07-22 (bare पिता read as biological father): re-anchored to the exalted compound परमपिता (Supreme Father) — pulled from how Hindi Scripture renders 'God our Father' (Rom 1:7 परमेश्वर हमारे पिता) and 'Our Father in heaven' (Mt 6:9). Unmistakably divine, still filial. NEVER revert to परमेश्वर/यहोवा

Hindi reviewer feedback 2026-07-22 (bare पिता read as biological father): परमपिता standing alone as the divine person (परमपिता, पुत्र, पवित्र आत्मा) — the qualifier is built into the word, so it cannot read as an earthly father
Same filial word without the possessive; स्वर्गीय keeps the register of the Lord's Prayer while पिता keeps it a father, not a deity.
Retains the Aramaic address of Mark 14:36 / Rom 8:15 / Gal 4:6 as the Hindi Bible does, glossed by the filial पिता — the intimate cry of a child.
REVERSION TOKEN — the traditional non-filial word for God-as-God; the contrast term, used only where English keeps 'God', never as a stand-in for पिता.
REVERSION TOKEN — the covenant name as in the Hindi OT; use only for 'the LORD'. Generic 'Lord' for Jesus = प्रभु (Prabhu), a distinct word.
Standard Hindi Bible term, confirmed in preview (John 3).
Relational, by the Father's name — not the fixed title पवित्र आत्मा — so the filial stem पिता is preserved (Gen 1:2).
Standard Hindi Bible title, confirmed in preview (John 3).
The Son (Jesus) as the counterpart of पिता in the Father–Son relation of John's Gospel.
Christological title with 'God' retained; native reviewer to confirm vs the filial alternative पिता का पुत्र ('the Son of the Father').
The exact John 3:16 wording of the Hindi Bible (अपना एकलौता पुत्र), confirmed in preview.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially: पिता का राज्य replaces परमेश्वर का राज्य, keeping the filial stem (John 3:3,5).
Standard Hindi Bible form, confirmed in preview.
Standard Hindi Bible term for Christ (John 3:28).
Transliterated title form, kept distinct in display from मसीह (Christ); reviewer to confirm the two-form split.
Standard Hindi Bible term (नई वाचा = New Covenant).
The Hindi Bible's word for hesed (Ps 136 refrain: उसकी करुणा सदा की है); reviewer to confirm vs the literal अटल प्रेम ('unwavering love').
For racham/splanchnizomai — the gut-and-womb tenderness the Gospels render 'तरस खाया'; alt अनुकंपा, reviewer to confirm.
Standard Hindi Bible term (Romans).
The John 3:16 wording of the Hindi Bible, confirmed in preview.
The 'new birth' of John 3:3,7; classic Hindi phrasing नये सिरे से जन्मना ('to be born afresh'), confirmed in preview.
